status: exact strict-Laguerre/Wiener equivalence with generic-kernel guard
proof_boundary: This artifact composes positive-boundary attainment with the first Laguerre correlation identity and Wiener's theorem to give an RH-equivalent translation-density target for every 0<t<=1/2. It supplies an exact smooth strict-log-concavity and positive-definiteness countermodel with double Fourier zeros. It does not prove the Xi correlation transform zero-free, translation density, RH, or Lambda<=0.

exact.strict_laguerre_equivalence: Lambda<=0 if and only if L_t(x)>0 for every real x and every 0<t<=1/2. The forward implication uses the simple real-zero factorization for t>Lambda; the reverse implication uses the finite multiple zero of H_Lambda forced when Lambda>0.
exact.factorization_identity: When H_t has simple real roots r_j, L_t(x)/H_t(x)^2=sum_j 1/(x-r_j)^2 away from roots, and L_t(r_j)=H_t'(r_j)^2>0.
exact.origin_sign: H_t(0)>0, H_t'(0)=0, H_t''(0)=-integral_0^infinity u^2*phi_t(u)du<0, hence L_t(0)>0.

wiener_density.statement: For K in L1(R), the linear span of its translates is dense in L1(R) if and only if its Fourier transform has no real zero.
wiener_density.application: Translations of K_(1,t) are dense iff L_t has no real zero. Since L_t(0)>0, zero-freeness is equivalent to L_t(x)>0 for all x.

exact.density_rh_equivalence: Lambda<=0 if and only if, for every 0<t<=1/2, the translations of K_(1,t) are dense in L1(R).
exact.positive_definite_scope: At a hypothetical boundary H_Lambda is Laguerre-Polya, so L_Lambda>=0 and K_(1,Lambda) is positive definite; a multiple zero c still gives Fourier[K_(1,Lambda)](2c)=L_Lambda(c)=0. Positive definiteness alone does not provide the required zero-freeness.

published_correlation_source.scope: Correlation kernels represent generalized Laguerre expressions; positive definiteness gives nonnegativity, not strict positivity.

strict_logconcavity_guard.conditional_curvature: With p_x(y) proportional to T_a(y)G_sigma(x-y), (log K)''=Var_x(Y)/sigma^4-1/sigma^2
strict_logconcavity_guard.strict_bound: Since Y lies in [-a,a], Var_x(Y)<=a^2; for sigma>a, (log K)''<=(a^2-sigma^2)/sigma^4<0.
strict_logconcavity_guard.double_zeros: xi=2*pi*n/a for every nonzero integer n
strict_logconcavity_guard.positive_definite: The transform is nonnegative, so K_(a,sigma) is positive definite.
strict_logconcavity_guard.translation_failure: The double Fourier zeros make its translates non-dense by Wiener.
strict_logconcavity_guard.scope_warning: This Gaussian-tail model blocks promotion from strict log-concavity and positive definiteness alone; it does not reproduce the Xi tail.

exact.nonpromotion_decision: Pointwise positivity, evenness, smoothness, strict log-concavity, and positive definiteness of a kernel do not force a zero-free Fourier transform. The Xi route needs a stronger correlation property such as Wiener translate density, strict Fourier positivity, or an Xi-specific total-positivity factorization.
exact.open_handoff: Prove uniformly for every 0<t<=1/2 that Fourier[K_(1,t)] has no real zero, equivalently that translations of K_(1,t) are dense in L1(R). A viable proof must use Xi-specific structure beyond generic strict log-concavity and positive definiteness.

rows.claim: A positive Newman boundary forces a zero of the first Laguerre expression.
rows.proof_boundary: Uses the finite positive-boundary attainment lemma.

rows.claim: Strict first-Laguerre positivity throughout positive time is equivalent to the Newman upper direction.
rows.formula: Lambda<=0 if and only if L_t(x)>0 for every real x and every 0<t<=1/2. The forward implication uses the simple real-zero factorization for t>Lambda; the reverse implication uses the finite multiple zero of H_Lambda forced when Lambda>0.
rows.proof_boundary: All real x and the full continuum 0<t<=1/2 are required.

diagnostics.factorization: When H_t has simple real roots r_j, L_t(x)/H_t(x)^2=sum_j 1/(x-r_j)^2 away from roots, and L_t(r_j)=H_t'(r_j)^2>0.
diagnostics.origin: H_t(0)>0, H_t'(0)=0, H_t''(0)=-integral_0^infinity u^2*phi_t(u)du<0, hence L_t(0)>0.

rows.claim: The first Laguerre expression is exactly the Fourier transform of one positive Xi correlation kernel.
rows.proof_boundary: Fubini and the midpoint/difference change of variables.

rows.claim: Wiener translate density is equivalent to zero-freeness of the correlation transform.
rows.formula: Translations of K_(1,t) are dense iff L_t has no real zero. Since L_t(0)>0, zero-freeness is equivalent to L_t(x)>0 for all x.
rows.proof_boundary: Classical Wiener L1 Tauberian theorem; K_(1,t) is integrable.

diagnostics.statement: For K in L1(R), the linear span of its translates is dense in L1(R) if and only if its Fourier transform has no real zero.
diagnostics.application: Translations of K_(1,t) are dense iff L_t has no real zero. Since L_t(0)>0, zero-freeness is equivalent to L_t(x)>0 for all x.

rows.claim: The missing Newman direction is equivalent to an all-positive-time correlation-density theorem.
rows.formula: Lambda<=0 if and only if, for every 0<t<=1/2, the translations of K_(1,t) are dense in L1(R).
rows.proof_boundary: Equivalent target only; density is not proved.

rows.claim: Positive definiteness yields nonnegative Laguerre expressions but does not exclude a boundary zero.
rows.formula: At a hypothetical boundary H_Lambda is Laguerre-Polya, so L_Lambda>=0 and K_(1,Lambda) is positive definite; a multiple zero c still gives Fourier[K_(1,Lambda)](2c)=L_Lambda(c)=0. Positive definiteness alone does not provide the required zero-freeness.

diagnostics.scope: Correlation kernels represent generalized Laguerre expressions; positive definiteness gives nonnegativity, not strict positivity.

rows.claim: A smooth positive even strictly log-concave positive-definite kernel can have double Fourier zeros.
rows.proof_boundary: Exact Gaussian-tail convolution model, not the Xi kernel.

diagnostics.conditional_curvature: With p_x(y) proportional to T_a(y)G_sigma(x-y), (log K)''=Var_x(Y)/sigma^4-1/sigma^2
diagnostics.strict_bound: Since Y lies in [-a,a], Var_x(Y)<=a^2; for sigma>a, (log K)''<=(a^2-sigma^2)/sigma^4<0.
diagnostics.double_zeros: xi=2*pi*n/a for every nonzero integer n
diagnostics.positive_definite: The transform is nonnegative, so K_(a,sigma) is positive definite.
diagnostics.translation_failure: The double Fourier zeros make its translates non-dense by Wiener.
diagnostics.scope_warning: This Gaussian-tail model blocks promotion from strict log-concavity and positive definiteness alone; it does not reproduce the Xi tail.

rows.claim: Generic kernel shape properties cannot be promoted to Wiener density.
rows.formula: Pointwise positivity, evenness, smoothness, strict log-concavity, and positive definiteness of a kernel do not force a zero-free Fourier transform. The Xi route needs a stronger correlation property such as Wiener translate density, strict Fourier positivity, or an Xi-specific total-positivity factorization.
rows.proof_boundary: Does not rule out stronger Xi tail or total-positivity structure.

rows.claim: It is sufficient to prove zero-freeness of the first Xi correlation transform for all positive times up to 1/2.
rows.formula: Fourier[K_(1,t)](xi)=L_t(xi/2)>0 for all xi in R, 0<t<=1/2

rows.claim: The next route should attack translate density using Xi-specific correlation structure.
rows.formula: Prove uniformly for every 0<t<=1/2 that Fourier[K_(1,t)] has no real zero, equivalently that translations of K_(1,t) are dense in L1(R). A viable proof must use Xi-specific structure beyond generic strict log-concavity and positive definiteness.
rows.proof_boundary: Not a proof of density, RH, or Lambda<=0.
